paper_title: Coral Reef Analysis and Isotopic Studies

methods.description: Aerial and diving surveys to locate and classify coral formations along the Pacific Costa Rican coast.

steps.description: Coral formations were initially located by means of aerial surveys from a light, fixed-wing aircraft.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct aerial surveys to locate coral formations

steps.description: Nearly vertical color photographs of each reef were obtained at 900 m to 1,200 m elevations, employing a polaroid filter to reduce glare.
steps.searchable_summary: capture vertical color photographs of reefs

steps.description: Coral formations along the NW Costa Rican coast were surveyed by aircraft in April 1973; coral formations present from the Gulf of Nicoya south to the Panama border were surveyed in February and April 1975.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct aircraft surveys of coral formations in specified regions

steps.description: Coral formations were classified as coral reefs or coral communities sensu Wainwright (1965).
steps.searchable_summary: classify coral formations as reefs or communities

methods.description: Estimation of coral cover and diversity using chain transect sampling.

steps.description: Coral cover and diversity were estimated from chain transect sampling.
steps.searchable_summary: use chain transect sampling to estimate coral cover and diversity

steps.description: Each chain transect, 10 m in length and with 73 links per m, was laid out so as to conform with the bottom irregularities.
steps.searchable_summary: lay out chain transect to conform with bottom irregularities

steps.description: Points in contact with each link were identified and numbered.
steps.searchable_summary: identify and number contact points on transect

methods.description: Measurement of coral growth and structure using X-ray and physical measurements.

steps.description: Annual linear skeletal growth in P. gigantea was estimated from the thickness of density bands measured with calipers and rule directly on X-ray negative film.
steps.searchable_summary: measure skeletal growth from X-ray film

steps.description: Two to three 5 mm-thick slabs were cut along the main growth axis of each coral and these were X-rayed in cardboard holders.
Medial colony growth was determined by measuring about 10 band couplets per colony.
steps.searchable_summary: measure band couplets to determine colony growth

methods.description: Analysis of coral samples for radiocarbon dating and stable isotopic composition.

steps.description: Corals collected from the dead reefs for isotopic analyses were excavated from the reef flat.
steps.searchable_summary: excavate coral samples for isotopic analysis

steps.description: The coral samples were air dried initially, then cleaned by scraping to assure complete removal of calcareous boring and encrusting organisms.
Radiocarbon was measured using gas proportional counting techniques.
steps.searchable_summary: measure radiocarbon using gas proportional counting

steps.description: The 0 13 C value was determined for reburned acetylene and the net count rate for each sample was corrected for isotope fractionation.
